title: Ensure private DNS zones for private endpoints that connect  to Batch accounts are configured.

input_row.title: Ensure private DNS zones for private endpoints that connect  to Batch accounts are configured.
input_row.description: Private DNS zones for Azure Batch private endpoints provide secure internal name resolution, preventing public internet exposure. When a private endpoint is created for a Batch account, Azure requires a private DNS zone (privatelink..batch.azure.com) to map the Batch service’s domain name to a private IP address within your virtual network (VNet).
input_row.rationale: To enable secure and private access to Azure Batch accounts, private DNS zones must be properly configured for private endpoints so as not to expose them publicly and allow for internal name resolution. Proper configuration of DNS provides assurance that DNS resolves to private IPs, reducing data exposure risk and support for security policy adherence. In the absence of proper DNS configuration, Batch services are open to connectivity failure, job interruption, or misconfigured public internet routing. Well- meshed private DNS zones hold traffic within the virtual network as intended, as a Zero Trust architecture and regulatory standard would dictate. Organizations need to audit and automate the configurations for secure and stable Batch processing.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Login to Azure portal https://portal.azure.com 2. Navigate to Batch accounts in the Azure portal For each batch account perform the following: 1. Expand Settings then click on Networking 2. Click the Private access tab, If no Private endpoints exist, the configuration fails this audit procedure. For each Private endpoint perform the following: 1. Click the name of the Private Endpoint 2. Expand Settings then click on DNS Configuration 3. Scroll to the bottom where a table of Configuration entries is found Ensure that there is an entry populated in the Private DNS Zone column (e.g. privatelink.batch.azure.com) Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/batch/private-connectivity#azure-policy • Policy ID: 4ec38ebc-381f-45ee-81a4-acbc4be878f8 - Name: 'Azure Batch accounts should use private DNS zones for 'private endpoints''
input_row.remediation: NOTE: This instruction assumes a Private Endpoint already exists for the Batch Account, and that a Private DNS Zone has already been created. Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Login to Azure portal https://portal.azure.com 2. Navigate to Batch accounts in the Azure portal For each batch account perform the following: 1. Expand Settings then click on Networking 2. Click the Private access tab, [If no Private Endpoints exist, one must be created before proceeding. Instruction for configuring Private Endpoints on Batch Accounts can be found in the recommendation titled "Ensure to Configure Batch accounts with private endpoints"] For each Private endpoint perform the following: 1. Click the name of the Private Endpoint 2. Expand Settings then click on DNS Configuration 3. Click the + Add Configuration button 4. Select each field appropriately (e.g. Private DNS zone "privatelink.batch.azure.com" - other zone names in additional information below) and enter a custom Configuration Name if desired 5. Click the Add button then allow a moment for deployment Refresh the DNS Configuration page, then scroll down and ensure an entry exists (e.g. "privatelink.batch.azure.com") under the Private DNS zone column in the Configuration entry table.
input_row.impact: NOTE: This recommendation assumes that a Private DNS Zone already exists. If one has not yet been created for Batch accounts [e.g. privatelink.batch.azure.com], that must be completed before it can be assigned to a Batch account's Private Endpoint. Network architecture must be carefully considered when deploying Private DNS Zones. DNS Zones should be used to associate like services. Private DNS zones should not be used to resolve public endpoints.
input_row.references: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private-link/private-endpoint-dns 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/batch/private-connectivity 3. Commercial Zone Names: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private- link/private-endpoint-dns#compute 4. Government Zone Names: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private- link/private-endpoint-dns#compute-1 5. China Zone Names: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private-link/private- endpoint-dns#compute-2
input_row.default_value: Azure DNS zones are not configured by default.
input_row.additional_information: Zone Names for resource type: Azure Batch (Microsoft.Batch/batchAccounts) Azure Commercial Cloud: 1. Subresource: batchAccount • Private DNS Zone name: privatelink.batch.azure.com • Public DNS zone forwarders: .batch.azure.com 2. Subresource: nodeManagement • Private DNS Zone name: privatelink.batch.azure.com • Public DNS zone forwarders: .service.batch.azure.com Azure Government Cloud: 1. Subresource: batchAccount • Private DNS Zone name: privatelink.batch.usgovcloudapi.net • Public DNS zone forwarders: .batch.usgovcloudapi.net 2. Subresource: nodeManagement • Private DNS Zone name: privatelink.batch.usgovcloudapi.net • Public DNS zone forwarders: .service.batch.usgovcloudapi.net Azure China Cloud: 1. Subresource: batchAccount • Private DNS Zone name: privatelink.batch.chinacloudapi.cn • Public DNS zone forwarders: .batch.chinacloudapi.cn 2. Subresource: nodeManagement • Private DNS Zone name: privatelink.batch.chinacloudapi.cn • Public DNS zone forwarders: .service.batch.chinacloudapi.cn

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
Based on the information provided in the JSON input:
- The audit section includes a reference to Azure Policy, which can be automated.
- The Azure Portal steps can be translated into API calls using Azure SDK or CLI, making it feasible to check for private DNS zone configurations programmatically.
- The rationale emphasizes that proper DNS configurations hold traffic within the network and reduce data exposure risk, which aligns with standard configurations that can be validated through Azure APIs.
- The availability of the references directs us to Azure's official documentation supporting automation.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_batch_private_endpoint_dns_zone_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use Azure SDK's `azure.mgmt.batch` to access Batch account configurations and private endpoints.
- Query for Batch accounts and their associated private endpoints within the Azure subscription.
- Specifically validate the DNS configuration to check if it includes an entry for the private DNS zone `privatelink.batch.azure.com` (or the respective zone based on cloud environment).
- Azure CLI command example:
  ```bash
  az batch account show --name <batch_account_name> --resource-group <resource_group_name>
  ```
  Followed by further commands to list private endpoints and their DNS configuration:
  ```bash
  az network private-endpoint show --name <private_endpoint_name> --resource-group <resource_group_name>
  ```
- Check for expected configurations:
  - Ensure at least one private endpoint exists.
  - Validate that the `Private DNS Zone` column contains the correct configuration entry (e.g., `privatelink.batch.azure.com`).
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass**: The private DNS zone is configured correctly with the expected entries.
  - **Fail**: No private DNS zone entries or incorrect entries present in the configuration.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is appropriate. The use of Azure APIs, Azure SDK for Python, and Azure CLI makes it feasible to automate the verification of private DNS zone configurations for Batch accounts.
- Azure SDK for Python and Azure CLI provide the necessary capabilities to query Batch accounts and their associated private endpoints, making this check automatable.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 7

- The automation details are generally correct, but there are some areas that could be improved for clarity and completeness.
- The Azure CLI commands provided are valid for retrieving Batch account and private endpoint information. However, the example commands could be more specific in demonstrating how to check the DNS configuration directly.
- The program name `azure_batch_private_endpoint_dns_zone_configured` is descriptive but could be more specific to indicate the DNS aspect, such as `azure_batch_private_endpoint_dns_zone_validation`.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

1. **Azure SDK for Python:**
   - The review could benefit from including specific methods from the `azure.mgmt.network` library to query private DNS zones and validate their configurations directly.
   - Example: Use `PrivateDnsManagementClient` to list and verify DNS zone entries.

2. **Azure CLI Enhancements:**
   - Provide a complete command sequence to directly check DNS zone configurations, such as:
     ```bash
     az network private-dns zone list --resource-group <resource_group_name>
     az network private-dns record-set a list --zone-name privatelink.batch.azure.com --resource-group <resource_group_name>
     ```
   - This would help in directly validating the presence of the required DNS entries.

3. **Program Name Suggestion:**
   - Consider renaming the program to `azure_batch_private_endpoint_dns_zone_validation` for better clarity on the specific check being performed.

4. **Additional Considerations:**
   - Ensure the automation script accounts for different Azure environments (e.g., Azure Government, China) where DNS zone names might differ.
   - Consider using Azure Policy to enforce the presence of private DNS zones for Batch accounts, which can complement the audit process.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-founded, and the approach is technically feasible with the suggested improvements. The use of Azure APIs and CLI commands is appropriate for automation.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- **Modify:** Implement the suggested improvements for more comprehensive automation details and clarity in the program name. Include specific Azure SDK methods and more detailed CLI command sequences to directly verify DNS configurations.
- The overall approach remains Automated, but enhancing the technical details will improve the robustness and clarity of the audit process.
